ac_coverage.details: Story has exactly one AC (AC1). All four tasks map to AC1 and collectively implement it end-to-end. No AC is unmapped.

dependency_correctness.details: Dependency graph is a valid DAG. T1 has no deps; T2 and T3 both depend on T1; T4 depends on T2 and T3. Topological order [T1, T2, T3, T4] is valid. Dependencies reflect real implementation ordering: dirty-state detection must precede both the interceptor and dialog, which must both precede integration wiring.

granularity.details: 4 tasks for a 3-story-point user story. Well within the preferred 3–8 broad-task range. Each task represents a distinct logical phase (detection, interception, dialog, integration) rather than a micro-step. Task decomposition mirrors the natural dependency chain of the feature.

clarity.details: Each task has a concise title, a multi-point description that calls out discovery steps and implementation choices, explicit ac_mapping, dependencies, priority, complexity, and a concrete definition_of_done. No vague or contradictory tasks found.

notes: Plan correctly defers codebase-discovery work (whether existing guard/dialog/dirty-state service exists) to the software-engineer stage via definition-of-done discovery steps in T1, T2, and T3 — an appropriate pattern given that librarian confidence was zero for all four codebase queries. Testing strategy is internally consistent: Jest for pure logic (T1, T2), Cypress component test for Angular component (T3), smoke test for integration (T4).

metacognitive_context.decision_rationale: Ran all three programmatic gate scripts first; all exited 0, so rubric evaluation was straightforward confirmation. Manual rubric review focused on dependency semantic correctness (not just graph validity) and granularity appropriateness for a 3-point story.

alternatives_discarded.approach: Skip rubric review after gates pass
alternatives_discarded.reason_rejected: Gates confirm structural correctness but not semantic quality of descriptions or appropriateness of decomposition for story size.

tool_anomalies.anomaly: python binary not found on PATH; python3 required instead. All three scripts succeeded under python3.
